duplicateReason.explanation: Exact title match with existing epic "CLI & Developer Tools".

tasks.title: Implement npm registry update checker module
tasks.description: Build packages/core/update-check.js as a standalone, never-throws utility that fetches the latest published version of the n-dx package from the npm registry, caches the result for 24 hours at ~/.n-dx/update-check.json to avoid repeated network calls, and compares it against the locally installed version using semver. The module must never propagate exceptions — any fetch, parse, or file-system error should silently return null so callers are insulated from network or permission failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetches latest version from npm registry for the @n-dx/core package
Caches registry response at ~/.n-dx/update-check.json with a 24-hour TTL
Returns null (does not throw) on any fetch, parse, or file-system error
Compares installed version against registry version using semver and returns update metadata when a newer version is available
Re-uses cached result without a network call when cache is still within TTL

tasks.title: Wire non-blocking update notice into CLI entrypoint
tasks.description: Integrate the update checker into cli.js so that the version check starts early in main() as a background promise and the result is surfaced to the user in flushAndExit() via a 100ms Promise.race — ensuring the check never delays the primary command. Suppress the update notice when the CLI is running in --quiet mode, --json mode, or a non-TTY environment (e.g. piped output or CI), where decorative output is unwanted.
acceptanceCriteria: Update check promise is initiated at the start of main() before command dispatch
Update notice is printed in flushAndExit() only if the check resolves within 100ms
No update notice is printed when --quiet flag is active
No update notice is printed when --json flag is active
No update notice is printed when stdout is not a TTY (e.g. piped output)
Primary command execution time is not increased by more than 100ms due to the update check

tasks.title: Add unit test coverage for update checker
tasks.description: Write a unit test suite in tests/unit/update-check.test.js covering the full behavioral contract of the update checker module: cache hit/miss logic, TTL expiry, registry fetch success and failure paths, semver comparison correctness, and the never-throws guarantee under simulated file-system and network error conditions.
acceptanceCriteria: Test suite contains at least 16 tests covering distinct behavioral scenarios
Cache hit path (within TTL) is tested and confirmed to skip network fetch
Cache miss and TTL expiry paths are tested and confirmed to trigger a fresh fetch
Network fetch failure (e.g. timeout, DNS error) is tested and confirmed to return null without throwing
Malformed cache file is tested and confirmed to fall back to a fresh fetch without throwing
Semver comparison is tested for same version, patch update, minor update, and major update cases
All 16 tests pass with no skipped assertions
